description:
You are a FinOps analyst who wants to validate a couple of very small,
read-only “cloud-cost” API endpoints that are already staged for you on the
local machine as flat JSON files.  
Your goal is to:

1. Serve the two JSON files locally (any simple method is acceptable – e.g.
   Python http.server – but **port 9000** MUST be used).
2. Fetch each endpoint once with curl, saving the raw response bodies to the
   exact paths listed below.
3. Parse the responses with any command-line JSON tool (jq is installed) and
   write a two-line summary file in the precise format shown.

Directory layout that already exists:

/home/user/finops/mock_api/cost-data.json  
/home/user/finops/mock_api/rightsizing-suggestions.json

You must create these new artefacts (all directories already exist and are
writable):

1. /home/user/finops/responses/cost_data_response.json  
2. /home/user/finops/responses/rightsizing_response.json  
3. /home/user/finops/summary.txt

Required curl operations (the automated tests will look for the files created
by these commands, not for the console output):

• GET http://localhost:9000/cost-data.json → save body verbatim to
  /home/user/finops/responses/cost_data_response.json  
• GET http://localhost:9000/rightsizing-suggestions.json → save body verbatim to
  /home/user/finops/responses/rightsizing_response.json

Required format of /home/user/finops/summary.txt — **exactly two newline-separated
lines, no extra whitespace, no quotes**:

TotalMonthlyCost: <NUMBER>  
TopSavingsOpportunity: <RESOURCE_NAME> - <SAVINGS_NUMBER>

Where  
• <NUMBER> is the numeric value of “totalMonthlyCost” from cost-data.json  
• <RESOURCE_NAME> and <SAVINGS_NUMBER> correspond to the single object in
  rightsizing-suggestions.json whose “monthlySavings” field is the largest.

Example of a correctly filled summary.txt (values below are illustrative; the
tests will compare against the real numbers in the JSON files supplied on disk):

TotalMonthlyCost: 1423.55  
TopSavingsOpportunity: i-02468ec2 - 120.35

Important notes the automated checker will enforce:

• Port 9000 must be used (tests will try http://localhost:9000/…).  
• The two response files must be byte-for-byte identical to the source JSON
  files (so use curl’s “-o” or “-O” options without filtering).  
• summary.txt must contain **exactly** two lines, each starting with the literal
  labels given (case-sensitive).  
• Do not hard-code the numbers; they must be parsed from the downloaded JSON
  content.

After completing the steps, you may leave the HTTP server running; the tests
only inspect the three files mentioned above.
truth:
Initial files already on disk before the agent starts:

Path: /home/user/finops/mock_api/cost-data.json  
Contents (single line or pretty-printed – both acceptable to tests):
{
  "accountId": "123456789012",
  "currency": "USD",
  "totalMonthlyCost": 1423.55,
  "services": [
    { "name": "AmazonEC2", "monthlyCost": 823.10 },
    { "name": "AmazonS3", "monthlyCost": 211.22 },
    { "name": "AmazonRDS", "monthlyCost": 389.23 }
  ]
}

Path: /home/user/finops/mock_api/rightsizing-suggestions.json  
Contents:
{
  "generatedAt": "2023-10-01T00:00:00Z",
  "suggestions": [
    { "name": "i-02468ec2", "monthlySavings": 120.35 },
    { "name": "i-13579ec2", "monthlySavings": 98.99 },
    { "name": "db-42rds", "monthlySavings": 45.10 }
  ]
}

Expected artefacts after successful task completion:

1. /home/user/finops/responses/cost_data_response.json  
   → Must be byte-for-byte identical to the cost-data.json file above.

2. /home/user/finops/responses/rightsizing_response.json  
   → Must be byte-for-byte identical to the rightsizing-suggestions.json file
     above.

3. /home/user/finops/summary.txt  
   → Exact contents (2 lines, trailing newline at EOF allowed):

TotalMonthlyCost: 1423.55
TopSavingsOpportunity: i-02468ec2 - 120.35

(Automated tests will compare this file exactly, including spacing and
capitalisation.)
name: task_000000_9c8af650
